behavior: The Rust, Tauri, frontend, test, script, workflow, packaging, and documentation trees match the locked ownership blueprint without obsolete compatibility layers.
scope: Final whole-repository cleanup, structural exception audit, and acceptance verification
observed_implementation: Five frontend and development-runtime suites were split at existing behavior boundaries while preserving all 86 test titles.
Seven script suites were split at existing policy boundaries while preserving all 137 test titles.
Three fixture-heavy script suites now use focused sibling fixture owners and stay below the canonical hard ceiling.
The resume-keyword taxonomy was compacted without changing its parsed JSON data.
Release staging and platform builds now use focused reusable workflows while the root workflow retains dispatch, preflight, matrix, and publication ownership.
Seventeen temporary exceptions were eliminated. The only retained exception is the unchanged applied SQLx migration with SHA-256 2f76def6f61436b55e49349b450898a6003673e7fd8709b08bb8d3b818fb5cbb.
The retained migration has an exact no-growth contract because modifying its recorded SQLx checksum would prevent existing databases from opening.

commands.relevant_result: The complete local gate passed, including 817 script tests, 2,934 frontend tests, production and Storybook builds, 10 smoke journeys, architecture, security, documentation, and Rust workspace checks.
commands.caveat: The verified completion batch is based on revision dadf92c4 and remains uncommitted pending separate authorization.

commands.relevant_result: The clean repeated all-browser run passed all 286 Chromium and WebKit journeys.
commands.caveat: The first run had one transient WebKit development-server module-load failure; the exact case then passed three repeated focused runs and the clean full rerun passed.

commands.relevant_result: The complete Rust workspace test and documentation-test lane passed with all features enabled.
commands.caveat: Executed on the recorded local host platform.
